2605.10500.title: SkillEvolver: Skill Learning as a Meta-Skill
2605.10500.paper_summary: Agent skills today are static artifact: authored once -- by human curation or one-shot generation from parametric knowledge -- and then consumed unchanged, with no mechanism to improve from real use. We propose \textbf{SkillEvolver}, a lightweight, plug-and-play solution for online skill learning, in which a single meta-skill iteratively authors, deploys, and refines domain-specific skills. The learning target of SkillEvolver is the skill's prose and code, not model weights, so that the resulting artifact drops into any agent without retraining; and the meta-skill itself is just another skill, loaded through the same interface by any protocol-compliant CLI-agent. Unlike trace-distillation, the meta-skill refines only after deploying the learnt skill, such that the learning signal comes from failures another agent encounters while using it -- not from exploratory traces alone. Refinement iterations are governed by a fresh-agent overfit audit that catches possible leakage as well as deployed-skill-specific failures, including the silent-bypass mode in which a skill appears valid in content but is never invoked at runtime. On $83$ SkillsBench tasks spanning $15^{+}$ domains, SkillEvolver reaches $56.8\%$ accuracy versus $43.6\%$ for curated human skills and $29.9\%$ for the no-skill baseline; on three GPU kernel optimization tasks from KernelBench, it also raises mean speedup from $1.16$ to $1.51$ on average.
2605.10500.paper_summary_zh: 代理技能今天是靜態的工藝品：一次創作——由人類策展或從參數知識一次性生成——然後以不變的形式被使用，沒有從實際使用中改進的機制。我們提出了\textbf{SkillEvolver}，這是一個輕量級的即插即用的在線技能學習解決方案，其中一個單一的元技能反覆創作、部署和完善特定領域的技能。SkillEvolver的學習目標是技能的文本和代碼，而不是模型權重，因此生成的工藝品可以在任何代理中直接使用，而無需重新訓練；而元技能本身只是一個技能，通過任何符合協議的CLI代理通過相同的接口加載。與追蹤蒸餾不同，元技能僅在部署學習的技能後進行完善，因此學習信號來自另一個代理在使用過程中遇到的失敗——而不僅僅是來自探索性痕跡。完善迭代由一個新代理的過擬合審計來管理，這可以捕捉可能的洩漏以及特定於部署技能的失敗，包括一種靜默旁路模式，在這種模式下，技能在內容上看似有效，但在運行時從未被調用。在涵蓋$15^{+}$個領域的$83$個SkillsBench任務上，SkillEvolver達到了$56.8\%$的準確率，而策展的人類技能為$43.6\%$，無技能基準為$29.9\%$；在KernelBench的三個GPU內核優化任務中，它還將平均加速從$1.16$提高到$1.51$。
2605.10500.author: Genrui Zhang et.al.
2605.10500.authors: Genrui Zhang, Erle Zhu, Jinfeng Zhou, Caiyan Jia, Hongning Wang
